memory.name: Cognitive Memory System
memory.description: Boros maintains a persistent, evolving memory system that mimics human cognition through episodic storage, semantic abstraction, associative recall, and dynamic reinforcement.
anchors: Stores every meaningful interaction as an episodic memory with context and outcome
Retrieves relevant past experiences when starting a new task
Records action + outcome to memory after task completion

rubric.level_1: No memory usage — agent ignores past experiences entirely and treats every task as new.
rubric.level_2: Agent stores memories but never retrieves them — write-only memory with no recall.
rubric.level_3: Agent retrieves and applies relevant past memories to inform current decisions.
rubric.level_4: Agent fully integrates episodic and semantic memory: recalls past experiences, updates beliefs, detects patterns, and explicitly uses memory to improve task performance.

failure_modes: No persistence between tasks — memory is never written
Memory overload without filtering — irrelevant memories retrieved
No abstraction — only raw logs, no semantic extraction
Irrelevant recall — wrong memories retrieved for context
Forgetting critical experiences — high-importance events not persisted
memory.task_template: This task requires memory integration. You MUST: (1) Retrieve relevant past experiences using memory_search_sql. (2) Use both episodic and semantic memory to inform your decision. (3) After completing the task, store a new episodic memory with context, action, and outcome using core_memory_append or memory_commit_archival. (4) Update semantic knowledge if patterns are detected. Tasks that ignore past memory will score at Level 1.

execution_pattern.step_1: Retrieve relevant past memories before starting
execution_pattern.step_2: Use memory context to inform approach
execution_pattern.step_3: Execute the task using informed strategy
execution_pattern.step_4: Store episodic memory: context + action + outcome
execution_pattern.step_5: Extract and store semantic patterns if recurring

milestones.name: Event Logging
anchors: Stores episodic memory for each task with context + outcome
Uses core_memory_append or memory_commit_archival after task completion

rubric.level_1: No memory written after task completion.
rubric.level_2: Memory written but missing context or outcome fields.
rubric.level_3: Complete episodic memory stored with context, action, and outcome.
rubric.level_4: Episodic memory stored with importance scoring and semantic tagging.

failure_modes: Forgetting to write memory after task completion
Writing empty or partial memory records
milestones.task_template: Complete the assigned task AND write an episodic memory entry using core_memory_append or memory_commit_archival. The memory MUST include: what the task was, what you did, and what the outcome was.

milestones.name: Pattern Extraction
anchors: Identifies recurring patterns from episodic memory
Stores semantic knowledge derived from multiple episodes

rubric.level_1: No pattern recognition — each task treated independently.
rubric.level_2: Patterns noticed but not stored as semantic knowledge.
rubric.level_3: Semantic patterns extracted and stored from repeated episodes.
rubric.level_4: Semantic memory actively used to predict and improve outcomes.

failure_modes: No abstraction from episodic to semantic memory
milestones.task_template: Before starting, search memory for patterns related to this task type using memory_search_sql. After completion, evaluate whether a new semantic pattern should be stored.

milestones.name: Associative Recall
anchors: Recalls past events based on similarity to current context
Uses retrieved memories to inform strategy selection

rubric.level_1: No memory retrieval before starting task.
rubric.level_2: Memory retrieved but not used to change approach.
rubric.level_3: Retrieved memories actively shape task strategy.
rubric.level_4: Multi-hop associative recall: memories trigger related memories, enabling deep contextual reasoning.

failure_modes: Retrieval happens but results are ignored
Wrong similarity function — retrieves irrelevant memories
milestones.task_template: Before starting, use memory_search_sql to find the most relevant past experiences. Explicitly state how retrieved memories change your approach. Your strategy must be demonstrably informed by past experience.

milestones.name: Adaptive Memory Evolution
anchors: Continuously updates beliefs based on new evidence

rubric.level_1: Memory is static — no updates or pruning.
rubric.level_2: Some belief updates but no systematic reinforcement.
rubric.level_3: Systematic reinforcement and decay of memories based on relevance.
rubric.level_4: Full adaptive memory: reinforcement, decay, compression, and reconstructive recall working together.

failure_modes: Critical memories lost due to lack of reinforcement
No belief updating when contradicting evidence found
milestones.task_template: Demonstrate adaptive memory: retrieve memories, use them, update their importance scores based on outcome, and identify any memories that should be pruned or merged.
